header_normalization: trim, collapse internal whitespace/newlines to a single space, uppercase

unit_mrp.reason: Kraus's source file has no per-unit MRP column at all -- only a line-total 'Transaction Value at MRP' and 'Transaction Quantity'. Confirmed 2026-07-18: no other column carries a per-unit price. Derived as mrp_value / quantity (see apps.ingestion.derivations.unit_mrp_from_mrp_and_quantity), unlike every other brand where unit MRP is supplied directly.

validation_rules.is_return_when: quantity < 0 OR mrp_value < 0 (matches every other brand's convention; confirmed in the 63-row sample: return rows have both quantity and mrp_value negative, invoice_no carries an 'R' infix e.g. '0202R-000625')
validation_rules.divide_by_zero_guard: if mrp_value == 0, discount_pct is null, not an error
notes: Unlike Killer/Pepe/Junior Killer, this file has no shared column vocabulary at all -- built from scratch by inspecting the actual sample file (Kraus Sales.xlsx, 63 rows, single store 'The Bombay Fashion' / location '02'), not a client-supplied mapping document.

ARTICLE NO has only 4 distinct values in the sample (L TOP, L TROUSER, L DENIM, L CARGO PANT) -- clearly a garment type, not a real per-SKU code, so it maps to category. The actual per-SKU code is STYLE NAME (e.g. LTA-2338), mapped to article_code. Client-confirmed 2026-07-18.
No sub_category-equivalent column exists in this file -- left unmapped, same as any brand that doesn't supply an optional dimension.

EOSS SCHEME NAME is a promo-scheme label (e.g. 'B1-30,B2-40,KRAUS' = a buy-1/buy-2 discount tier), not an actual fashion season like SS23/AW23 -- client-confirmed 2026-07-18 to leave it unmapped (captured to extra for audit) rather than mismap it onto season.

TOTAL DISCOUNT AMT (not ITEM DISCOUNT AMT alone) is mapped to discount_value: in the sample data BILL DISCOUNT AMT is always 0 so the two are identical, but TOTAL is the one that's actually documented as bill+item combined, so it's the correct source if a future file ever has a nonzero bill-level discount. ITEM/BILL amounts are kept in extra for audit.

TOTAL DISCOUNT PCT is already a plain percentage (e.g. 10.00 meaning 10%), unlike Pepe's WAD column which is a fraction (0.30...) needing fraction_to_percent -- cast as a plain decimal here, not fraction_to_percent.

SIZE NAME is a combined field (e.g. '26 / S', or occasionally 'S / W-86CM') with no consistent single delimiter/order across rows -- stored as-is in size rather than attempting to split it into two fields.

SUPPLIER NAME (always 'SOUL INDIA (PEPE)' in the sample) and BRAND NAME (always 'KRAUS') are redundant with the (brand, product_line) upload context that selects this config -- not treated as authoritative, captured to extra for audit only, same convention as Killer's BRAND column.

TRANSACTION TYPE is 'Retail Sales' for every sampled row -- no other value observed yet; captured to extra rather than assumed constant, since a future file could carry an exchange/other transaction type this config hasn't seen.

This is a small (63-row), single-store sample file, not a multi-month/multi-store historical export like the other three brands' original samples -- re-verify these assumptions (especially the return-row sign convention and unit_mrp derivation) once a larger real file is available.
